sections.label: Section 1.1237
sections.original: τί δʼ ἔστιν ;
sections.translation: What do you mean ?

sections.label: Section 1.1238
sections.original: σιγᾶν ἄμεινον , μή τις ἔνδοθεν κλύῃ .
sections.translation: It is better to be silent so that no one inside may hear .

sections.label: Section 1.1239
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐ μὰ τὴν ἄδμητον αἰὲν Ἄρτεμιν ,
sections.translation: No , by ever-virgin Artemis ,

sections.label: Section 1.1240
sections.original: τόδε μὲν οὔ ποτʼ ἀξιώσω τρέσαι ,
sections.translation: I will never think it right to tremble before eternally house-bound women , that useless burden on the ground !

sections.label: Section 1.1243
sections.original: ὅρα γε μὲν δὴ κἀν γυναιξὶν ὡς Ἄρης
sections.translation: Yes , but remember that Ares dwells in women , too . You know this well by experience , I believe .

sections.label: Section 1.1245
sections.original: ὀτοτοτοτοῖ τοτοῖ ,
sections.translation: oh , no ! ah , me ! You have reminded me of my sorrow , one which by its nature cannot be veiled ,

sections.label: Section 1.1250
sections.original: οἷον ἔφυ κακόν .
sections.translation: cannot be done away with , cannot be forgotten !

sections.label: Section 1.1251
sections.original: ἔξοιδα , παῖ , ταῦτʼ · ἀλλʼ ὅταν παρουσία
sections.translation: I know this , too ; but when occasion prompts , we must recall those crimes .

sections.label: Section 1.1253
sections.original: ὁ πᾶς ἐμοί ,
sections.translation: Each moment of all time , as it comes , would be a proper occasion

sections.label: Section 1.1255
sections.original: τάδε δίκᾳ χρόνος ·
sections.translation: for me to make these just complaints . Scarcely now have I had my lips set free .

sections.label: Section 1.1257
sections.original: ξύμφημι κἀγώ · τοιγαροῦν σῴζου τόδε .
sections.translation: Yes , I agree ; therefore guard your freedom .

sections.label: Section 1.1258
sections.original: τί δρῶσα ;
sections.translation: What must I do ?

sections.label: Section 1.1259
sections.original: οὗ μή ʼστι καιρὸς μὴ μακρὰν βούλου λέγειν .
sections.translation: When it is inopportune , do not want to speak too much .

sections.label: Section 1.1260
sections.original: τίς οὖν ἂν ἀξίαν γε σοῦ πεφηνότος
sections.translation: No , who could exchange due silence for speech , when you have appeared ? For now my eyes have seen you , beyond all thought and hope !

sections.label: Section 1.1264
sections.original: τότʼ εἶδες , εὖτε θεοί μʼ ἐπώτρυναν μολεῖν
sections.translation: You saw me when the gods moved me to come . ⋯

sections.label: Section 1.1265
sections.translation: You have told me of a grace higher still than the first , if a god brought you to our house ;

sections.label: Section 1.1270
sections.original: αὐτὸ τίθημʼ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: I acknowledge in it the work of the divine .

sections.label: Section 1.1271
sections.original: τὰ μέν σʼ ὀκνῶ χαίρουσαν εἰργαθεῖν , τὰ δὲ
sections.translation: On the one hand I hesitate to curb your gladness , but on the other I fear that you may be overwhelmed by too much joy .

sections.label: Section 1273
sections.original: ἰὼ χρόνῳ μακρῷ φιλτάταν ὁδὸν
sections.translation: O you who , after such a long time , saw fit to make your most happy journey and appear to me ,

sections.label: Section 1275
sections.original: μή τί με , πολύπονον ὧδʼ ἰδὼν
sections.translation: do not , now that you have seen me in all my misery —

sections.label: Section 1276
sections.original: τί μὴ ποήσω ;
sections.translation: What should I not do ?—

sections.label: Section 1277
sections.original: μή μʼ ἀποστερήσῃς
sections.translation: — Do not rob me of the comfort of your face ; do not force me to forego it !

sections.label: Section 1278
sections.original: τῶν σῶν προσώπων ἁδονὰν μεθέσθαι .
sections.translation: I would be angry , indeed , if I saw another attempt it .

sections.label: Section 1279
sections.original: ἦ κάρτα κἂν ἄλλοισι θυμοίμην ἰδών .
sections.translation: You give your consent ?

sections.label: Section 1280
sections.original: ξυναινεῖς ;
sections.translation: Why would I not ?

sections.label: Section 1281
sections.original: τί μὴν οὔ ;
sections.translation: My friends , I heard a voice that I could never have hoped to hear ; nor could I have restrained my emotion in silence and without a cry , when I heard it .

sections.label: Section 1285
sections.original: τάλαινα . νῦν δʼ ἔχω σε · προυφάνης δὲ
sections.translation: ah , me ! But now I have you . You have appeared with that dear face , which I could never , even in misery , forget .

sections.label: Section 1288
sections.original: τὰ μὲν περισσεύοντα τῶν λόγων ἄφες ,
sections.translation: Spare all superfluous words , and inform me neither of our mother ’ s wickedness ,

sections.label: Section 1290
sections.original: μήθʼ ὡς πατρῴαν κτῆσιν Αἴγισθος δόμων
sections.translation: nor how Aegisthus drains the wealth of our father ’ s house — what part he pours on the ground and what he squanders at random . For the story would preclude you from the opportunity afforded by the moment . Instead tell me that which will suit our present circumstances : where , either openly or in ambush ,

sections.label: Section 1295
sections.original: γελῶντας ἐχθροὺς παύσομεν τῇ νῦν ὁδῷ .
sections.translation: we may put an end to our enemies ’ laughter by means of my coming . Make sure that our mother does not discover you by your radiant face , when we two have gone into the house . Rather , make lament , as if for the feigned disaster . For when we succeed , then

sections.label: Section 1300
sections.original: χαίρειν παρέσται καὶ γελᾶν ἐλευθέρως .
sections.translation: there will be opportunity to rejoice and exult in freedom .

sections.label: Section 1301
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ὦ κασίγνηθʼ , ὧδʼ ὅπως καὶ σοὶ φίλον
sections.translation: Brother , be assured that my conduct will be as pleases you , since all my joy derives from you , and is not my own . Nor would I consent

sections.label: Section 1305
sections.original: αὐτὴ μέγʼ εὑρεῖν κέρδος · οὐ γὰρ ἂν καλῶς
sections.translation:
to win a great good for myself at the cost of the slightest pain to you . For in doing so I would not honorably support the divine power that attends us now .
But you know how matters stand here . I do not doubt it . You must have heard that Aegisthus is away from home , but that our mother is inside . And never fear that she

sections.label: Section 1310
sections.original: γέλωτι τοὐμὸν φαιδρὸν ὄψεται κάρα .
sections.translation: will ever see my face lit up with smiles . My old hatred of her has been welded to my heart , and since I have seen you , for very joy I will never cease to weep . How indeed could I stop when I have seen you come home on this one day first as dead ,

sections.label: Section 1315
sections.original: καὶ ζῶντʼ ἐσεῖδον ; εἴργασαι δέ μʼ ἄσκοπα ·
sections.translation: and then in life ? What you have done to me is inconceivable — so much so that , if my father were to return to me alive , I would no longer think it a portent , but would believe that I truly saw him . Therefore now that you have come to me by such a path , command me as your spirit bids you . For had I been alone ,

sections.label: Section 1320
sections.original: οὐκ ἂν δυοῖν ἥμαρτον · ἢ γὰρ ἂν καλῶς
sections.translation: I would not have failed in one of two things : a noble salvation , or a noble destruction .

sections.label: Section 1322
sections.original: σιγᾶν ἐπῄνεσʼ ὡς ἐπʼ ἐξόδῳ κλύω τῶν ἔνδοθεν χωροῦντος .
sections.translation: Silence ! I hear someone walking inside as if to come out .

sections.label: Section 1325
sections.original: δόμων ἀπώσαιτʼ οὔτʼ ἂν ἡσθείη λαβών .
sections.translation: could either turn away from these doors , or rejoice at receiving .

sections.label: Section 1326
sections.original: ὦ πλεῖστα μῶροι καὶ φρενῶν τητώμενοι ,
sections.translation: Utterly foolish and senseless children ! Are you weary of your lives , or is there no wit inborn in you , that you do not see how you stand not on the brink , but in the very midst

sections.label: Section 1330
sections.original: τοῖσιν μεγίστοις ὄντες οὐ γιγνώσκετε ;
sections.translation: of immense danger ? Had I not long kept watch at these doors , your doings would have been in the house before your bodies . But as it was , I put myself the task of averting that .

sections.label: Section 1335
sections.original: καὶ νῦν ἀπαλλαχθέντε τῶν μακρῶν λόγων
sections.translation: Be done now with your long speeches and this insatiable shouting for joy , and go inside . In dealings of this sort delay is harmful , but the time is ripe for being done .

sections.label: Section 1339
sections.original: πῶς οὖν ἔχει τἀντεῦθεν εἰσιόντι μοι ;
sections.translation: Then how will things inside stand when I enter ?

sections.label: Section 1340
sections.original: καλῶς · ὑπάρχει γάρ σε μὴ γνῶναί τινα .
sections.translation: All is well . The fact is that no one will know you .

sections.label: Section 1341
sections.original: ἤγγειλας , ὡς ἔοικεν , ὡς τεθνηκότα .
sections.translation: You have reported me as dead , I presume ?

sections.label: Section 1342
sections.original: εἷς τῶν ἐν Ἅιδου μάνθανʼ ἐνθάδʼ ὢν ἀνήρ .
sections.translation: Know that here you are a man numbered with the shades .

sections.label: Section 1343
sections.original: χαίρουσιν οὖν τούτοισιν ; ἢ τίνες λόγοι ;
sections.translation: Do they rejoice , then , at the news ? Or what do they say ?

sections.label: Section 1344
sections.original: τελουμένων εἴποιμʼ ἄν · ὡς δὲ νῦν ἔχει ,
sections.translation: I will tell when all is completed . Meanwhile ,

sections.label: Section 1345
sections.original: καλῶς τὰ κείνων πάντα , καὶ τὰ μὴ καλῶς .
sections.translation: all is favorable for us on their part , even that which is not favorable .

sections.label: Section 1346
sections.original: τίς οὗτός ἐστʼ , ἀδελφέ ; πρὸς θεῶν φράσον .
sections.translation: Who is this , brother ? By the gods , tell me .

sections.label: Section 1347
sections.original: οὐχὶ ξυνίης ;
sections.translation: Do you not know who he is ?

sections.label: Section 1347a
sections.original: οὐδέ γʼ ἐς θυμὸν φέρω .
sections.translation: No , nor can I imagine .

sections.label: Section 1348
sections.original: οὐκ οἶσθʼ ὅτῳ μʼ ἔδωκας εἰς χέρας ποτέ ;
sections.translation: Do you not know the man to whose hands you gave me once ?

sections.label: Section 1349
sections.original: ποίῳ ; τί φωνεῖς ;
sections.translation: What man ? What do you mean ?

sections.label: Section 1349a
sections.original: οὗ τὸ Φωκέων πέδον
sections.translation: I mean him , by whose hands and through your forethought

sections.label: Section 1350
sections.original: ὑπεξεπέμφθην σῇ προμηθίᾳ χεροῖν .
sections.translation: I was secretly conveyed away to Phocian soil .

sections.label: Section 1351
sections.original: ἦ κεῖνος οὗτος , ὅν ποτʼ ἐκ πολλῶν ἐγὼ
sections.translation: Is this he in whom alone out of many I once found a true ally at the time of our father ’ s murder ?

sections.label: Section 1353
sections.original: ὅδʼ ἐστί · μή μʼ ἔλεγχε πλείοσιν λόγοις .
sections.translation: This is he ; question me no further .

sections.label: Section 1354
sections.original: ὦ φίλτατον φῶς , ὦ μόνος σωτὴρ δόμων
sections.translation: O joyous day ! O sole preserver of Agamemnon ’ s house ,

sections.label: Section 1355
sections.original: Ἀγαμέμνονος , πῶς ἦλθες ; ἦ σὺ κεῖνος εἶ ,
sections.translation: how did you come here ? Are you indeed the man who saved my brother and myself from many sorrows ? O dearest hands , O messenger whose feet were kindly servants ! How could you be with me so long and remain unknown , without giving a ray of illumination ,

sections.label: Section 1360
sections.original: λόγοις ἀπώλλυς , ἔργʼ ἔχων ἥδιστʼ ἐμοί ;
sections.translation: but instead afflicting me with stories , while possessed of sweetest reality ? Welcome , Father , for it is a father that I seem to behold ! Welcome , and know that in one day I have hated you and loved you as no man ever before !
